confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_78ed9462-2da0-4f64-b3fc-67ef396c147e read back as received

containment: Treat any binding created by ambient foreground after diagnosis as suspect unless independently confirmed.
Use explicit HWND binding only for repair until tool is hardened.
corrective_action: Remove or disable ambient foreground binding as a mutating path.
Require explicit -Hwnd for identity binding mutation.
If UI picker is used, it must capture target HWND before opening and pass that explicit HWND through to mutation.
Persist requested HWND, observed HWND, title/class/process evidence, and asserted identity in mutation evidence.

incident.intended_target_basis: previously observed current operator surface HWND
incident.unsafe_mechanism: Set-FocusedWindowIdentityBinding.ps1 called without -Hwnd, using GetForegroundWindow at mutation time

preventive_action: Rename commands so dangerous focus-based binding cannot be invoked casually.
Add tests proving binding without explicit HWND is refused.
Add preflight warning when multiple live Windows Terminal surfaces exist and no explicit HWND is supplied.
Make OSM/window-label repair workflows use captured-HWND discipline by construction.
payload.summary: An operator-surface identity repair attempted to bind narada-andrey.Kevin by calling Set-FocusedWindowIdentityBinding.ps1 without an explicit HWND. Between diagnosis and mutation, foreground focus changed, so the command bound the identity to the wrong operator surface. This is a recurrence-prone authority/mutation defect: ambient active-window state is not a stable target for identity binding.
payload.title: CAPA: prohibit ambient foreground identity binding for operator surfaces
verification: Attempt Set-FocusedWindowIdentityBinding without -Hwnd and verify refusal.
Bind a fixture HWND explicitly and verify only that HWND changes identity binding.
Run a simulated focus-drift test where foreground changes between capture and mutation; mutation must still target captured HWND or fail.
